paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Now these are the names of the sons of Israel, who came into Egypt (every man and his household came with Jacob):

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, and Judah,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Issachar, Zebulun, and Benjamin,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Dan and Naphtali, Gad and Asher.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And all the souls that came out of the loins of Jacob were seventy souls: and Joseph was in Egypt already.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Joseph died, and all his brethren, and all that generation.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the children of Israel were fruitful, and increased abundantly, and multiplied, and waxed exceeding mighty; and the land was filled with them.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Now there arose a new king over Egypt, who knew not Joseph.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he said unto his people, Behold, the people of the children of Israel are more and mightier than we:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: come, let us deal wisely with them, lest they multiply, and it come to pass, that, when there falleth out any war, they also join themselves unto our enemies, and fight against us, and get them up out of the land.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Therefore they did set over them taskmasters to afflict them with their burdens. And they built for Pharaoh store-cities, Pithom and Raamses.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But the more they afflicted them, the more they multiplied and the more they spread abroad. And they were grieved because of the children of Israel.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the Egyptians made the children of Israel to serve with rigor:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and they made their lives bitter with hard service, in mortar and in brick, and in all manner of service in the field, all their service, wherein they made them serve with rigor.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the king of Egypt spake to the Hebrew midwives, of whom the name of the one was Shiphrah, and the name of the other Puah:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and he said, When ye do the office of a midwife to the Hebrew women, and see them upon the birth-stool; if it be a son, then ye shall kill him; but if it be a daughter, then she shall live.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But the midwives feared God, and did not as the king of Egypt commanded them, but saved the men-children alive.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the king of Egypt called for the midwives, and said unto them, Why have ye done this thing, and have saved the men-children alive?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the midwives said unto Pharaoh, Because the Hebrew women are not as the Egyptian women; for they are lively, and are delivered ere the midwife come unto them.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God dealt well with the midwives: and the people multiplied, and waxed very mighty.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And it came to pass, because the midwives feared God, that he made them households.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Pharaoh charged all his people, saying, Every son that is born ye shall cast into the river, and every daughter ye shall save alive.
